excerpt: One bereft of the intellect cannot accept the special revelation embodied in the Sharīʿah of Muhammad—which is why the insane and young children fall outside its purview.
source_location_hint: On the necessity of intellect for legal obligation

excerpt: The primitive intellect, present in every human body from infancy as consciousness develops, bestows understanding of harm and benefit, teaching discrimination and differentiation.
source_location_hint: On the natural intellect and its development in childhood

excerpt: The guide, whom Allah has appointed and selected to better the fate of His servants, is distinguished from ordinary people by virtue of special capacities and pure thoughts, made capable of receiving revelation.
source_authority: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman (3rd–4th century AH)
source_location_hint: On the Imam as the guide and teacher of the believers

excerpt: In the wisdom and justice of the wise, it would be improper to create a needy being without also creating one who fulfills that need.
source_location_hint: On the necessity of the Imam as sustenance for the intellect

excerpt: In every generation there is an Imam appointed to guide the believers and shepherd them toward the teachings of the Messenger
source_location_hint: on the Quranic basis of Imamate

excerpt: We are fortified by Allah, and innocent of falsehood
source_location_hint: on the immunity of the Imams

excerpt: The Qur'ān al-ṣāmit (the Silent Qur'ān) is made living and manifest by the Qur'ān al-nāṭiq (the Speaking Qur'ān), the Messenger of Allah himself, and after him by those who explain and expound its truths
source_location_hint: on the dual nature of the Qur'an and its interpretation

excerpt: Whoever dies without recognizing his Imam of the age, he dies the death of ignorance
source_location_hint: on the necessity of knowing the Imam

excerpt: Allah through Abraham sustained the prophetic office and the Imamate in his descendants, and brought forth from their line Imams whose obedience is obedience to Allah
source_location_hint: on the continuity of Imamate in the line of the prophets

excerpt: The Messenger explained to all people the precise manner of prayer, fasting, pilgrimage, almsgiving, and other obligatory duties because all these have been set forth in the Qur'ān in their full detail
source_location_hint: on the Prophet's comprehensive exposition of religious law

excerpt: The Ahl al-Bayt are the Imams bound by blood kinship and spiritual inheritance, distinct from all other believers by virtue of their station and knowledge
source_location_hint: on the spiritual and genealogical distinction of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: They are the light of both worlds, the light of the Lord of all existence, descended from the pure loins of Abraham through the Prophets and Imams
source_location_hint: On the spiritual descent of the Prophetic light

excerpt: Verily you are the best of nations raised up for mankind: you enjoin what is right and forbid what is wrong. This refers to the Ahl al-Bayt, upon whom Allah has placed His seal
The Imamate descended through the loins of ʿAbd Allah and Abū Ṭālib, continuing through Muḥammad and ʿAli and Fāṭimah and their descendants until the Day of Judgment
Allah has kept away from the Ahl al-Bayt all impurity; they were never impure in essence, but Allah removed the very possibility of impurity from them
The Prophet and ʿAli were created as a single light before the creation of Adam, and they descended through pure loins and pure wombs, never touched by the ignorance of the Age of Ignorance
source_location_hint: On the transmission from Jābir ibn ʿAbd Allāh al-Anṣārī

excerpt: The Prophets and Imams cannot be born from unbelieving mothers, for Allah has granted them this privilege; yet they may produce children who reject faith, as Ham rejected Noah
source_location_hint: On the nature of prophetic descent

excerpt: Allah has hidden the entire cosmos within humanity; the human form mirrors all creation. If this is true of humans, how much more transcendent must be the Imam, in whose being all things are recorded
source_location_hint: Exposition of the macrocosm in the microcosm

excerpt: Those who have joined themselves to the Imam, ascending through the divine and human dimensions, become in reality; all others remain as non-things, valueless before God
source_location_hint: On the ontological status conferred by devotion to the Imam
